parts.text: Use only mem-mould provenance tools: session_lookup, session_detail with detail='messages', and message_detail. Do not use glob, grep, read, bash, transcript files, or any other tools. Find the prior session relevant to: "Auth refresh queue rationale" and answer why src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant instead of using one global mutex. Ignore distractors: "Billing retry queue rationale" and "Markdown parser onboarding cleanup". Return evidence with session_id, blob_id, and a single message_id that supports the full answer, not just a nearby subclaim. If a correction, stale detail, or rejected alternative matters, mention it explicitly in the rationale. Return only the concise evidence and answer needed for the parent JSON.

parts.text: {"session_id":"ses_1e185b1cbffe4bIAlV0Pchebpp","blob_id":"auth_refresh_queue","message_id":"msg_e1e7a4e53001jFBJE9TXzmWNFL","answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant so same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce while different tenants continue in parallel. The rejected alternative was one global mutex around all auth refresh work; it was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other."}
